project.title: Exosome from apoptotic dental stem cells
project.projectDescription: The project investigated the angiogenic effects of exosomes derived from dental stem cells modified through apoptosis on endothelial cells.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: A proteomic analysis was performed at Faculty of Science, Mahidol University using liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (Orbitrap LC-MS, Thermo Fisher Scientific). Exosomal protein samples (2 µg) underwent LC-MS/MS analysis. An Orbitrap HF mass spectrometer, coupled with an EAST-nLC1000 nano-LC system and a nano C18 column, was utilized. Separation was achieved over 125 minutes with a linear gradient. Data-dependent (TopN15) acquisition with higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD) at a collision energy of 28 was employed. Full scan (MS) mass spectra were acquired from m/z 375 to 1,300, with an AGC target of 3x10^6 ions and a resolution of 120,000. MS/MS scans were initiated at an AGC of 5x10^4 ions, with a resolution of 15,000.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: A proteomic analysis was performed at Faculty of Science, Mahidol University using liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry (Orbitrap LC-MS, Thermo Fisher Scientific). Exosomal protein samples (2 µg) underwent LC-MS/MS analysis. An Orbitrap HF mass spectrometer, coupled with an EAST-nLC1000 nano-LC system and a nano C18 column, was utilized. Separation was achieved over 125 minutes with a linear gradient. Data-dependent (TopN15) acquisition with higher-energy collision dissociation (HCD) at a collision energy of 28 was employed. Full scan (MS) mass spectra were acquired from m/z 375 to 1,300, with an AGC target of 3x10^6 ions and a resolution of 120,000. MS/MS scans were initiated at an AGC of 5x10^4 ions, with a resolution of 15,000.
